text: **Anna:** Hi Pete, what are you {doing|している|/ˈduːɪŋ/|What are you doing today?}?
**Pete:** Oh, hi Anna.
**Pete:** Right now, I am {fixing|修理している|/ˈfɪksɪŋ/|I am fixing my bicycle.} my car.
**Anna:** How's it going?
**Pete:** It's going not so {good|うまく|/ɡʊd/|Everything is good today.}
**Anna:** How can I help?
**Anna:** I was {planning|計画している|/ˈplænɪŋ/|I am planning a trip.} to visit some friends, but if you need help, I can help.
**Anna:** I like {helping|助けること|/ˈhɛlpɪŋ/|I like helping my friends.}
**Pete:** Anna, I can fix it {myself|自分で|/maɪˈsɛlf/|I can do it myself.}
**Pete:** But thanks.
**Anna:** Pete, I think I found your {problem|問題|/ˈprɒbləm/|We have a big problem.}
**Anna:** These are {spark plugs|点火プラグ|/spɑːrk plʌɡz/|Spark plugs are in cars.}
**Anna:** They start the {engine|エンジン|/ˈɛndʒɪn/|The car engine is running.}
**Pete:** I know that, Anna, but there were too many {spark plugs|点火プラグ|/spɑːrk plʌɡz/|Spark plugs are important.}
**Pete:** in there.
**Pete:** So I took out the extra ones.
**Anna:** There are no extras, Pete.
**Anna:** You need all of them.
**Dan:** Pete, can you {fix|修理する|/fɪks/|Can you fix my bike?} a car yourself?
**Pete:** Well, Anna, last night I was watching the online {video course|ビデオコース|/ˈvɪdioʊ kɔːrs/|I watched an online course.}, You Can Fix a Car Yourself.
**Dan:** Yes, you can fix your car yourself
**Pete:** And I watched the first ten {lessons|レッスン|/ˈlɛsənz/|I have three lessons today.}, so I think I know what I'm doing.
**Anna:** How many lessons are there?
**Anna:** That'll take too {long|長い時間|/lɔːŋ/|This will take a long time.}
**Anna:** If I fix your car, I'll have it {running|走っている|/ˈrʌnɪŋ/|The car is running smoothly.} in 10 to 15 minutes.
**Anna:** Where are your {tools|工具|/tuːlz/|I need some tools.}?
**Pete:** I have this.
**Pete:** And this.
**Anna:** Great.
**Anna:** Um, I'll get my {tools|道具/工具|/tuːlz/|These are my favorite tools.}
**Anna:** I'll be back in a {flash|瞬く間に|/flæʃ/|I'll be back in a flash.}
**Pete:** But Anna, I don't need your help
**Anna:** Pete, when I was a {teenager|十代の人|/ˈtiːneɪdʒər/|I was a teenager in the 90s.}, I was {fixing|修理する|/ˈfɪksɪŋ/|I'm fixing my bicycle.} cars myself.
**Anna:** I learned from a {master|名人/達人|/ˈmæstər/|He is a master of chess.}
**Anna:** You can trust me.
**Pete:** Okay.
**Anna:** Great, but we need {teamwork|チームワーク|/ˈtiːmwɜːrk/|Teamwork is important in sports.}
**Anna:** You sit in the car.
**Anna:** When I say go, you start the {engine|エンジン|/ˈendʒɪn/|The car engine is running.}
**Anna:** Okay, go.
**Anna:** Stop.
**Anna:** Okay, go.
**Anna:** Huh.
**Pete:** It's not starting.
**Anna:** What's wrong?
**Pete:** This car is {stupid|馬鹿な/愚かな|/ˈstuːpɪd/|That was a stupid mistake.}
**Anna:** Stupid, stupid!
**Anna:** Pete, {kicking|蹴る|/ˈkɪkɪŋ/|Stop kicking the ball.} the tires will not help.
**Pete:** Well, you did not help, Anna.
**Pete:** You did not help!
**Anna:** Pete, Pete, pull yourself {together|まとまる/落ち着く|/təˈgɛðər/|Pull yourself together and focus.}, man!
**Anna:** Give me the keys.
**Anna:** I must feel the key in the {ignition|イグニッション|/ɪɡˈnɪʃən/|Turn the key in the ignition.} and turn it myself.
**Master:** Use the key, Anna.
**Master:** Turn the key, Anna.
**Master:** What are you {forgetting|忘れる|/fərˈgɛtɪŋ/|I'm forgetting my keys again.}, Anna?
**Anna:** Pete!
**Anna:** What?
**Anna:** You are out of {gas|ガソリン|/ɡæs/|The car is out of gas.}
**Anna:** I can't be {out of|～がない/不足している|/aʊt əv/|I'm out of milk.} gas.
**Anna:** I'm {out of|～がない/不足している|/aʊt əv/|I'm out of money.} gas.
**Anna:** Sorry, Anna.
**Anna:** I'll go {get|取りに行く/入手する|/get/|I'll get some water.} some.
**Anna:** Pete.
**Anna:** You {relax|リラックスする/くつろぐ|/rɪˈlæks/|Please relax and enjoy.}
**Anna:** {Clean|きれいにする/洗う|/kliːn/|Clean your room now.} your face.
**Anna:** You can watch the rest of your {online|オンラインの|/ˈɒnlaɪn/|Online classes are popular.} video course.
**Anna:** It'll be {faster|より速い|/ˈfæstər/|Bike is faster than walking.} if I go.
**Anna:** In a {flash|瞬間/一瞬|/flæʃ/|I'll be back in a flash.}
**Anna:** Till next time
